duplicate_check.absorption_strategy: extend existing native systems and migrate plugin semantics into schemas, runtime models, editor panels, diagnostics, and tests

source_semantics: capture item gates capture attempt
enemy/species capture metadata maps to actor id
successful capture removes enemy from battle
successful capture suppresses exp/gold/drop reward
map and battle BGM can be selected from a configured pool
selection is deterministic from a seed
empty and non-positive-weight tracks are ignored
battle presentation can define looping animated media layers
media layers support background/foreground/overlay roles, playback rate, opacity, region override, and fade-in frames
battle presentation can define battler light cues with radius, color, opacity, pulsate, flicker, and death-hide policy
blood splatter is a first-class native battle VFX cue kind
blood VFX round-trips through timeline project data and runtime effect cues
map presentation can define parallax layers with map/screen lock, blend mode, opacity, scroll rates, region mask, and draw order
map presentation can define procedural smoke emitters by region with intensity, size, opacity, color, bloom, and drift
map terrain can define region mesh height, curve, and walkability rules
map camera can define edge-scroll, keyboard pan, click-follow, speed, margin, and recenter hotkey
fast travel destinations can carry map transfer targets, preview assets, descriptions, unlock flags, locked common-event fallbacks, and current-location hiding
fast travel preview returns deterministic transfer commands and diagnostics for locked, missing, or current-location-hidden destinations
vehicle profiles can carry allowed terrain tags, BGM/BGS assets, speed and encounter multipliers, interior transfer targets, and transition metadata
vehicle preview returns deterministic movement, audio, interior-transfer, and terrain-blocking diagnostics
map events can be marked draggable without requiring an RPG Maker plugin runtime
drag metadata supports free, horizontal, and vertical axes, grid snapping, passability policy, required switch gating, and drag bounds
event validation reports invalid axes, inverted bounds, bounds outside the owning map, and missing required switches
event authoring snapshots expose draggable event counts for editor state views
chip damage and chip healing previews are resolved natively from configurable percentages and minimums
zero-damage presentation can be configured as miss, evasion, immune, or no effect
buff and debuff preview levels clamp against configurable custom caps
troop member positions can reuse authored coordinates by enemy id when policy allows it
map, self, scoped, and JS variable/switch lanes are isolated from the global state hub while using the same native value type
nested message text escapes resolve recursively with a bounded depth guard
picture task bindings support high-count picture slots and common-event triggers
picture task validation reports unavailable slots and missing task/common-event ids
actors can have class-aware stat allocation pools with unspent level-up points
stat rules define point cost, stat gain, and caps for hp, mp, atk, def, mat, mdf, agi, and luk
allocation preview calculates before/after stats, spent points, remaining points, caps, and overspend diagnostics
editor snapshot exposes stat allocation preview state for progression panels
